name: G-stable
statement_text:
A one-leg method [α, β] is ‘G-stable’ if M given by (451e)
is positive semi-definite.

  We present the example of the BDF2 method with
                                     4   1      2
                    [α(z), β(z)] = 1 − z + z 2 ,   .

which is positive semi-definite if and only if G is the positive definite matrix

The relationship between the y and y sequences given by (452b) and (452d)
was suggested by Dahlquist (1976) as an indication that stability questions
for a linear multistep method can be replaced by similar questions for the
corresponding one-leg method.

variables.name: [α, β]
variables.definition: one-leg method defined by generating polynomials α(z) and β(z)

variables.name: G
variables.definition: positive definite matrix used in the norm ||·||_G

equations.content: M = [m_{ij}] where m_{ij} are coefficients from the identity involving G, α_i, β_i

preamble: The definition concerns G-stability for one-leg methods applied to nonlinear ODEs. The matrix M arises from an identity comparing differences between numerical solutions yn and zn, and its positive semi-definiteness ensures stability.

dependencies.name: stability in the sense of Dahlquist
